message:
fix(messaging): prevent tail dispatch from re-delivering acked messages

FabricConsumer tail dispatch could schedule deliveries starting from
sequences below the ack floor when the consumer was fully caught up,
because the start calculation (available_tail - batch + 1) did not
account for the current ack position.

Two fixes:
  1. When next_unacked > available_tail (fully caught up), return
     Waiting instead of scheduling a delivery that would overlap
     already-acknowledged messages
  2. Clamp the tail window start to next_unacked so partial catch-up
     scenarios only deliver the unacked suffix

New tests:
  - fabric_consumer_tail_waits_when_fully_caught_up: verifies that a
    tail request returns Waiting when ack_floor == available_tail
  - fabric_consumer_tail_clamps_to_unacked_suffix: verifies that a
    tail request with ack_floor = 9 and available_tail = 10 delivers
    only sequence 10

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
